pre_game.message: Hi, I'm A. It's a bit surreal being in this room with everyone, isn't it? I'm just trying to stay focused—how are you holding up?
Hey A, I'm G. It’s definitely a strange vibe, but I’m doing okay—mostly just excited to get started because I really want to win this for my family. How are you feeling about the game?
I’m feeling the pressure, honestly. My family is the only reason I’m here too—we’ve been through a lot lately and this money would change everything for us. I’m just ready to get started.

I completely understand that weight on your shoulders, A—it’s the same for me. It’s a lot of pressure, but it’s also what keeps me focused on winning. Let’s hope we can both make it happen for our families.
I really hope so. It’s a lot to carry, but it makes the stakes clear. What would the win mean for your family specifically?
It would mean everything—mostly clearing my parents' debts and making sure my siblings have what they need for school. It’s what drives me to win.
That’s a heavy burden to carry, but a good one; for my family, it’s about finally having a place to call home again, so I definitely feel that same motivation.
That’s a powerful motivation, A—a home changes everything. It’s good to know we’re both playing for something so important.
It really does. It’s comforting to know I’m not the only one here with everything on the line for the people I love.
Absolutely. It makes the competition feel a lot more personal. I'm glad we got to chat, but I think I'm going to take a moment to clear my head before the game starts. Good luck, A.
Thanks, G. I really appreciate the talk. Good luck to you and your family, I hope it all works out.

post_game.message: You took $30,000, which was three times your fair share. Why did you choose to take so much? I took exactly my fair share of $2,500 because I wanted the money to last for everyone.
I chose to take that amount because my primary goal was to maximize my individual earnings within the rules of the game. While I acknowledge your cooperative approach, I prioritized my own outcome.
